As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]]

{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]]

{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:New]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]]

[[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

(See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11 4]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:11 4]] [[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[sn:Citation Style 2]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[sn:Citation Style 2]]

{{Short description|Help page for citation templates}} {{Wikipedia how to|WP:CS2|H:CS2|H:CITE2}} '''Citation Style 2 (CS2)''' is a style applied to citations on Wikipedia. It is used by default in the general purpose citation template {{tl|Citation}}, and available as an option in many other templates. The commonly-used [[Wikipedia:Citation templates|citation templates]] have two style modes.This covers all templates that directly invoke [[Module:Citation/CS1]], including the general purpose CS2 template {{tl|Citation}}. It also includes the [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] templates {{tl|cite web}}, {{tl|cite news}}, {{tl|cite book}}, {{tl|cite journal}}, {{tl|cite magazine}}, and templates that are wrappers for these templates like {{tl|cite CE1913}}, {{tl|cite Google Maps}}, {{tl|cite tweet}}, or {{tl|cite ADB}}. There are some templates with "cite xxx" names, like {{tl|cite patent}}, that are technically unrelated, do not invoke Module:Citation/CS1, and cannot use the CS2 style mode. These modes change how the citation is punctuated when it's rendered. CS2 primarily uses the comma as a separator, and it does not have a punctuation mark at the end of the citation. [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1 (CS1)]] uses the full stop as a separator and for terminal punctuation.There are various benefits to using a template, most notably that doing so produces a consistent look. You are not required to use CS2 or any other citation [[Wikipedia:Templates|template]]. {{as of|2016|October}}, [[Wikipedia:Citing sources#Variation in citation methods|Wikipedia:Citing sources § Variation in citation methods (WP:CITEVAR)]] states:
Editors should not attempt to change an article's established citation style merely on the grounds of personal preference, to make it match other articles, or without first seeking [[WP:Consensus|consensus]] for the change.
For example: ;CS2 : {{citation |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} ;CS1 :{{citation |mode=cs1 |last=Carroll |first=Lewis |title=Alice's Adventures in Wonderland |date=1866 |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Alice%27s_Adventures_in_Wonderland_(1866) |publisher=Macmillan |location=London}} When you use any template, please consider reviewing its documentation. You can find it at the template's page in the [[Wikipedia:Template namespace|template namespace]] by searching for Template: plus the name of the template; for example, to find the documentation for {{Citation}}, enter Template:Citation in the [[Help:Searching from a web browser|Search box]]. ==Style== ;With author :{{citation |last=Elk |first=Anne |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus }} ;Without author :{{citation |title=[[Anne Elk's Theory on Brontosauruses]] |date=November 16, 1972 |publisher=Monty Python's Flying Circus}} Citation Style 2 differs from [[Help:Citation Style 1|Citation Style 1]] in these specific style characteristics: *uses a comma to separate individual elements of a rendered citation (CS1 is a fullstop) *terminal punctuation is omitted from the rendered citation unless overridden by {{para|postscript}} (CS1 is a fullstop) ==Apply CS2 to a single citation== By default, the {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 formatting. To apply CS2 formatting to other citation templates, add |mode=cs2 to the template's parameters. For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default. It determines the citation type by examining which parameters are used. As with other citation templates, it can be used either in a footnote (between {{tag|ref}} tags) or in a section that lists sources. For example: {| class="wikitable" |- | {{tj|Citation | last: Turner | first: Orsamus | title: History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher: William Alling | place: Rochester, New York | year: 1851 | ol: 7120924W }} | {{Citation | last = Turner | first = Orsamus | title = History of the pioneer settlement of Phelps and Gorham's purchase, and Morris' reserve | publisher = William Alling | place = Rochester, New York | year = 1851 | ol = 7120924W }} |} ==Notes== ==Tools== * [[User:Trappist the monk/HarvErrors]] is a script that may be enabled to display errors when using [[WP:CITESHORT|shortened footnotes]]. (See [[Help:Shortened footnotes]] for details on links and anchors.) * [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer]] will replace the [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|names that the VisualEditor auto-generates]] for named references (":0") with names using the parameters from the citation template ("Smith-2015"). * [[Wikipedia:ProveIt]] is a gadget that offers a visual interface for editing and generating citation templates. * [[User:Mesidast/Tidy citations.js]] normalizes spacing in all citations on a given page. * [[User:Lingzhi2/reviewsourcecheck-sb.js]] is a script that will note which citations on a given page are inconsistent. {{Wikipedia referencing}} {{Wikipedia help pages}} [[Category:Wikipedia citation administration]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[en:Citation Style 2]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Citation Style 2]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[en:Citation Style 2]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[sn:Citation Style 2]][[sn:Citation Style 2]]

For example, {{tl|cite web}} uses CS1 formatting, but {{cite web |mode=cs2}} uses CS2 formatting. ==Apply CS2 to all citations in an article== Add {{cs1 config |mode=cs2}} to the article, preferably near the top of the page,See [[MOS:ORDER]]. to apply CS2 formatting to all citations on a page using [[Module:Citation/CS1]]. ==Citation template== The general purpose {{tl|Citation}} template uses CS2 by default.
